name: Fedora Linux

pricing.details: Free and open source. Backed by Red Hat.

description: General-purpose Linux distribution with strong security defaults including SELinux enforcing mode, frequent package updates, and Wayland as the default display server.
rationale: Fedora is evaluated here as the reference mainstream Linux distribution for privacy-conscious users who need a practical daily driver. It ships with SELinux in enforcing mode, uses Wayland by default, and includes no proprietary telemetry. It is not privacy-hardened and does not route traffic anonymously. For users migrating from Windows or macOS, Fedora provides a substantial privacy improvement with comparable usability. Not suitable as a substitute for Tails or Qubes in high-risk contexts.
